evidence: MusiteDeep is a deep-learning predictor for protein PTM site prediction; it operates entirely within this task definition (per-residue binary classification per PTM type using a sequence window)

MusiteDeep's timestamp-based benchmark on 13 PTM types shows higher AUC and area-under-PR than ModPred (logistic regression) and the strongest classical per-PTM baseline (DeepPhos, GlycoEP, GPS-PAIL, MePred-RF, CSS-Palm4.0, UbiProber, GPS-SUMO, RF-Hydroxysite) on 12 of 13 PTM types under both metrics
Review's central organizing concept; the paper defines and frames multi-omics integration as joint ML+network-pharmacology pipeline.
Synthesis review collates qualitative case-study evidence that multi-omics + ML + network-pharmacology pipelines recover signals beyond single-omics analysis.
Paper coins the term 'Posttranslational Modification Inspired Drug Design (PTMI-DD)' and lays out its four operational strategies (covalent / induced-pocket / PPI / degrader).

Paper presents asciminib (ABL001) myristoyl-pocket allosteric Bcr-Abl inhibition, tazemetostat EZH2 inhibition, HDAC inhibitor approvals, thalidomide/CRBN degraders, and a self-reported covalent RhoA inhibitor as evidence that targeting PTM-modified isoforms yields better selectivity and expands the druggable space.
Atz et al. (2021) provide the canonical molecular-sciences review of GDL, defining the term and organizing the field around symmetry priors.
The review surveys SE(3)/E(3)-equivariant message-passing GNNs (TFN, EGNN, Cormorant, NequIP, PaiNN, SE(3)-Transformer) as a central GDL family for molecular modelling.
The review covers RNN- and transformer-based chemical language models on SMILES (and SELFIES, DeepSMILES) as one of the four representation classes of molecular GDL.

Review synthesizes evidence from quantum-chemistry, drug-discovery, and protein-structure-prediction literature that geometric priors improve molecular modelling, while documenting the inconsistent gain in drug discovery.
Review explicitly documents reliable equivariant-GNN gains for quantum chemistry but inconsistent gains for drug-discovery property prediction.
